D9 BiologyL4 AnalyzeReference

Bee UV Vision — Hidden Nectar Guides

honeybees see UV; many flowers display UV-absorbing centre patches invisible to us — landing strips that funnel pollinators straight to the nectar

Bee cones peak at 340 (UV), 430 (blue), and 530 nm (green) — no red. Many flowers (Mimulus, evening primrose, dandelion) reflect yellow uniformly to us, but the centre absorbs UV, contrasting sharply for bees and indicating where to land. The pattern is called a nectar guide or "bee landing strip".

Toggle human vs bee view. The same dandelion has an invisible bullseye in UV.
